panels.description: Total configured probe targets reported by netsonar_targets_total for the selected job.
Number of targets whose latest probe result has probe_success=1.
Number of targets whose latest probe result has probe_success=0. Zero is shown explicitly when there are no failures.
Average latest wall-clock probe duration across all targets for the selected job.

panels.title: Critical Failures
panels.description: Critical Failures counts targets with impact="critical" and probe_success=0.

panels.title: Timed Out
panels.description: Timed Out counts targets whose latest probe reached or exceeded its configured timeout.
NetSonar agent version reported by netsonar_build_info for the selected job. Use this to confirm which agent build is currently scraped and to spot mixed versions during rollouts.

panels.title: Config
panels.description: Current configuration hash from netsonar_config_info. Use this to verify that agents picked up the expected config after reloads or rollouts.

panels.title: Config Age
panels.description: Seconds since the latest successful NetSonar configuration reload for the selected job.

panels.title: All Probes — Status Table
panels.description:
Current status of every configured probe target. Sorted by Status so failures appear first.

Column legend:
- Status: latest probe outcome from probe_success. OK = succeeded, FAIL = failed.
- Timed Out: YES means the latest probe reached or exceeded its configured timeout. Blank means the latest result did not hit the timeout.
- Total Probe Time: wall-clock duration of the latest probe (probe_duration_seconds).
- Time Limit: effective configured per-target timeout in seconds (probe_timeout_seconds).
- Limit Used: latest Total Probe Time divided by Time Limit. This is a tuning signal, not the source of truth for timeout classification.
- Name / Probe / Target / Proxy / Proxy Endpoint: target identity and probe route. Proxy shows the configured proxy_name and stays blank for direct traffic. Proxy Endpoint is populated from netsonar_target_proxy_info for proxied targets.
- Service / Host / Port / Scope / Region / Partition / Account / Impact / Target Type: routing and ownership labels from the target config. Dynamic tag columns are shown once; duplicate columns from joined queries are hidden.

panels.title: Proxy Registry
panels.description: Current proxy_name to proxy_endpoint mapping for proxied targets. Targets is the number of active targets using each proxy.

panels.title: Skipped Probe Cycles
panels.description: Tracks scheduled probe cycles that NetSonar skipped because the previous run for the same target was still in progress. The scheduler runs at most one probe per target at a time; when a target duration exceeds or overlaps its configured interval, the stale tick is dropped and probe_skipped_overlap_total increments. Non-zero values usually mean the interval is too short for the probe duration, the timeout is too close to or longer than the interval, or the target/path is intermittently slow. This is a global scheduler health signal across all probe types, not an MTU-specific metric.
Total direct HTTP probe targets (probe_type="http", proxy_name="").
Direct HTTP targets whose latest probe result succeeded.

targets.expr: count(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name=""} == 0) OR on() vector(0)

panels.description: Direct HTTP targets whose latest probe result failed.
Average latest wall-clock duration for direct HTTP probes.

panels.title: HTTP Duration median (5m) (Direct)

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.5, probe_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name=""}[5m]) unless (probe_timed_out{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name=""} == 1)

panels.description: Median total duration over 5 minutes for direct HTTP probes only (proxy_name=""). Threshold lines are hidden to keep the panel visually clean; use the phase panels below for latency diagnosis.

panels.title: HTTP Status Codes (Direct)
panels.description: HTTP response status codes for direct-path HTTP probes. The series is present only when an HTTP response was received; connection, DNS, TLS, or timeout failures appear as no status-code sample.

panels.title: HTTP Response Truncated (Direct)
panels.description: 1 means the direct HTTP response body exceeded the effective response body limit. Truncation does not fail normal HTTP probes by itself. The series is present only when truncation could actually be evaluated from a received direct HTTP response.

panels.title: Slowest HTTP Probes (Direct, topk 10)
panels.description: Top 10 slowest direct HTTP probes by total duration. Use this to spot the slowest direct endpoints at a glance.

panels.title: HTTP Phase Breakdown (Direct)
panels.description:
Instant snapshot of direct HTTP phase durations pivoted by target.

Column legend:
- Target: HTTP target name; click to drill down to HTTP Details.
- Status: latest probe outcome from probe_success. OK = succeeded, FAIL = failed.
- dns_resolve: DNS lookup time for hostname targets. N/A means DNS was not observed, for example a literal IP target.
- tcp_connect: TCP connection establishment time.
- tls_handshake: Target TLS handshake time. N/A means TLS was not observed, for example plain HTTP.
- request_write: Time from connection/TLS readiness to request write completion. Usually near-zero for GET/small requests; grows when request_body_bytes sends an upload payload.
- ttfb: Time from request write completion to first response byte.
- transfer: Response body read time up to response_body_limit_bytes.
- total_phases: sum of observed phase timings for this target.

Only Status and total_phases use background color. Ordinary phase cells are uncolored; N/A means the phase was not observed or does not apply.

targets.expr: label_replace(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name=""}, "phase", "Status", "__name__", ".*") or probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name=""} or label_replace(sum by (target_name) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name=""}), "phase", "total_phases", "__name__", ".*")

panels.title: HTTP Total Duration (Direct, last)
panels.description: Horizontal bar gauge of total direct HTTP probe duration per target from probe_duration_seconds. This should be approximately equal to total_phases in HTTP Phase Breakdown (Direct), which sums probe_phase_duration_seconds for observed HTTP phases. Small differences are expected because probe_duration_seconds is the outer wall-clock probe duration, while total_phases includes only instrumented sub-phases and may exclude small prober/scheduler/metric overhead.

targets.expr: probe_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name=""} unless (probe_timed_out{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name=""} == 1)

panels.title: HTTP Phase Timing (Direct)
panels.description: Per-phase timing for direct HTTP probes. Missing phases mean the latest probe did not reach that phase or the phase does not apply to the target.
Total proxied HTTP probe targets (probe_type="http", proxy_name!="").
Proxy-path HTTP targets whose latest probe result succeeded.

targets.expr: count(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name!=""} == 0) OR on() vector(0)

panels.description: Proxy-path HTTP targets whose latest probe result failed.
Average latest wall-clock duration for proxied HTTP probes.

panels.title: HTTP Duration median (5m) (Proxy)

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.5, probe_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name!=""}[5m]) unless (probe_timed_out{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name!=""} == 1)

panels.description: Median total duration over 5 minutes for proxy-path HTTP probes only (proxy_name!=""). Threshold lines are hidden to keep the panel visually clean; use the phase panels below for latency diagnosis.

panels.title: HTTP Phase Breakdown (Proxy)
panels.description:
Instant snapshot of proxied HTTP phase durations pivoted by target and proxy. This panel shows the full HTTP flow through the proxy, not only the proxy hop.

Column legend:
- Target: HTTP target name; click to drill down to HTTP Details.
- Status: latest probe outcome from probe_success. OK = succeeded, FAIL = failed.
- Proxy: configured proxy_name used for this target path.

Proxy setup phases:
- proxy_dial: TCP connection to the proxy, including proxy hostname DNS when applicable.
- proxy_tls: TLS handshake with an HTTPS proxy. N/A for plain HTTP proxies.
- proxy_connect: CONNECT request/response with the proxy for HTTPS targets. N/A for plain HTTP targets through a forward proxy.

Target HTTP exchange phases:
- tls_handshake: TLS handshake with the target through the proxy tunnel for HTTPS targets.
- request_write: HTTP request write after proxy setup and target TLS readiness.
- ttfb: Time from request write completion to first response byte.
- transfer: Response body read time up to response_body_limit_bytes.
- total_phases: sum of observed phase timings for this target/proxy path.

request_write, ttfb, and transfer are expected for proxied HTTP because NetSonar still performs the target HTTP exchange after proxy setup completes. Only Status and total_phases use background color; ordinary phase cells are uncolored.

targets.expr: sum by (target_name, proxy_name, phase) (label_replace(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name!=""}, "phase", "Status", "__name__", ".*")) or sum by (target_name, proxy_name, phase) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name!=""}) or label_replace(sum by (target_name, proxy_name) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxy_name!=""}), "phase", "total_phases", "__name__", ".*")

panels.title: HTTP Phase Timing (Proxy)
panels.description: Per-phase timing for proxied HTTP probes. Includes proxy setup phases and target HTTP exchange phases; missing phases mean the latest probe did not reach that phase or the phase does not apply.

panels.title: HTTP Response Body Status per Target
panels.description: Current HTTP response-body probe status per target and path. Status is the latest probe outcome from probe_success. HTTP Code is present when an HTTP response was received. Body Match shows whether the response body matched the configured string or regex; N/A means body evaluation did not complete.

targets.expr: sum by (target_name, target, proxy_display, metric) (label_replace(label_replace(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name=""}, "metric", "Status", "__name__", ".*"), "proxy_display", "N/A", "proxy_name", ".*") or label_replace(label_replace(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name!=""}, "metric", "Status", "__name__", ".*"), "proxy_display", "$1", "proxy_name", "(.+)")) or sum by (target_name, target, proxy_display, metric) (label_replace(label_replace(probe_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name=""}, "metric", "Duration (s)", "__name__", ".*"), "proxy_display", "N/A", "proxy_name", ".*") or label_replace(label_replace(probe_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name!=""}, "metric", "Duration (s)", "__name__", ".*"), "proxy_display", "$1", "proxy_name", "(.+)")) or sum by (target_name, target, proxy_display, metric) (label_replace(label_replace((probe_http_status_code{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name=""} or on(target_name, proxy_name) (probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name=""} * 0 / 0)), "metric", "HTTP Code", "__name__", ".*"), "proxy_display", "N/A", "proxy_name", ".*") or label_replace(label_replace((probe_http_status_code{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name!=""} or on(target_name, proxy_name) (probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name!=""} * 0 / 0)), "metric", "HTTP Code", "__name__", ".*"), "proxy_display", "$1", "proxy_name", "(.+)")) or sum by (target_name, target, proxy_display, metric) (label_replace(label_replace((probe_http_body_match{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name=""} or on(target_name, proxy_name) (probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name=""} * 0 / 0)), "metric", "Body Match", "__name__", ".*"), "proxy_display", "N/A", "proxy_name", ".*") or label_replace(label_replace((probe_http_body_match{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name!=""} or on(target_name, proxy_name) (probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body", proxy_name!=""} * 0 / 0)), "metric", "Body Match", "__name__", ".*"), "proxy_display", "$1", "proxy_name", "(.+)"))

panels.description: Total HTTP response-body probe targets (probe_type="http_body").
HTTP response-body probes where body evaluation completed and matched the configured body_match_string or body_match_regex.
HTTP response-body probes where body evaluation completed but did not match the configured body expectation.
Average latest wall-clock duration for HTTP response-body probes.

panels.title: HTTP Response Body Match
panels.description: Shows whether the HTTP response body matches the configured pattern (body_match_string or body_match_regex). 1 = match, 0 = mismatch. The series is present only when body evaluation actually completed; request/response failures appear as no sample.

panels.title: HTTP Response Body Status Codes
panels.description: HTTP status codes for HTTP response-body probes. The series is present only when an HTTP response was received; request, DNS, TLS, or timeout failures appear as no status-code sample.

panels.title: HTTP Response Body Duration median (5m)
panels.description: Median total duration over 5 minutes for HTTP response-body probes. Threshold lines are hidden to keep the panel visually clean; use probe status, body-match, status-code, and phase panels for validation diagnosis.

panels.title: HTTP Response Body Phase Breakdown
panels.description: Instant snapshot of HTTP response body probe phase durations pivoted by target. Status is the latest probe outcome from probe_success. Direct probes can emit dns_resolve, tcp_connect, tls_handshake, request_write, ttfb, and transfer. Proxy-path probes can emit proxy_dial, optional proxy_tls, proxy_connect, tls_handshake, request_write, ttfb, and transfer. total_phases is the sum of observed phase timings for this target. Only Status and total_phases use background color; ordinary phase cells are uncolored. N/A cells mean the phase was not observed or does not apply.

targets.expr: sum by (target_name, phase) (label_replace(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body"}, "phase", "Status", "__name__", ".*")) or sum by (target_name, phase) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body"}) or label_replace(sum by (target_name) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="http_body"}), "phase", "total_phases", "__name__", ".*")

panels.title: HTTP Response Body Phase Timing
panels.description: Per-phase timing for HTTP response body probes. Includes direct and proxy-path phases; missing phases mean the latest probe did not reach that phase or the phase does not apply.
Total raw proxy CONNECT probe targets (probe_type="proxy_connect").
Proxy CONNECT targets whose latest probe result succeeded. With expected_proxy_connect_status_codes configured, success can mean the expected denial was observed.
Proxy CONNECT targets whose latest probe result failed.
Average latest wall-clock duration for raw proxy CONNECT probes.

panels.title: Proxy CONNECT Status per Target
panels.description:
Current status of each proxy CONNECT target. Sorted by Status so failures appear first.

Column legend:
- Status: binary probe outcome from probe_success. OK = proxy accepted the CONNECT tunnel or returned the expected CONNECT denial, FAIL = unexpected CONNECT status or connection failure.
- CONNECT Code: HTTP status returned by the proxy to the CONNECT request when available. Colors the kind of response: green (2xx) = tunnel established; orange (3xx-4xx) = client/policy response, typically an explicit denial such as 403 — paired with Status=OK only when the target's expected_proxy_connect_status_codes matches; red (5xx) = upstream/infrastructure failure; - = no CONNECT response observed (proxy unreachable or dial failed before any response).
- Name / Service / Target / Scope / Region: target identity and routing context from config labels.
- Duration (s): wall-clock time for the CONNECT probe, including TCP dial to proxy and CONNECT request/response. Use the Proxy CONNECT Phase Timing panel to see which stage is slow.

panels.title: Proxy CONNECT Duration
panels.description: Total wall-clock time for the CONNECT tunnel establishment per proxy target, including TCP dial to proxy and CONNECT handshake.

panels.title: Proxy CONNECT Phase Breakdown
panels.description: Instant snapshot of raw proxy CONNECT probe phase durations pivoted by target. Status is the latest probe outcome from probe_success. proxy_dial is the TCP connection to the proxy, proxy_tls is the TLS handshake with an HTTPS proxy, proxy_connect is the CONNECT request/response, and total_phases is the sum of observed CONNECT probe phases. Only Status and total_phases use background color; ordinary phase cells are uncolored. N/A cells mean the phase was not observed or does not apply.

targets.expr: label_replace(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="proxy_connect"}, "phase", "Status", "__name__", ".*") or probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="proxy_connect"} or label_replace(sum by (target_name) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="proxy_connect"}), "phase", "total_phases", "__name__", ".*")

panels.title: Proxy CONNECT Phase Timing
panels.description:
Phases observed in the latest CONNECT probe result. Shown for both successful and failed probes; missing phases mean the probe did not reach that phase.

Phase legend:
- proxy_dial: TCP connection to the proxy.
- proxy_tls: TLS handshake with the proxy, only for HTTPS proxy endpoints.
- proxy_connect: CONNECT request/response with the proxy. High values usually point to proxy latency or upstream tunnel establishment delay.

panels.title: TCP Duration — Local

targets.expr: probe_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="tcp", scope="local"} unless (probe_timed_out{job=~"$job", probe_type="tcp", scope="local"} == 1)

panels.description: TCP probe duration for targets tagged scope="local". TCP probes are connect-only: duration covers DNS resolution when needed plus TCP connection establishment.

panels.title: TCP Duration — External

targets.expr: probe_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="tcp", scope="external"} unless (probe_timed_out{job=~"$job", probe_type="tcp", scope="external"} == 1)

panels.description: TCP probe duration for targets tagged scope="external". TCP probes are connect-only: duration covers DNS resolution when needed plus TCP connection establishment.

panels.title: TCP Duration — All Targets
panels.description: TCP probe duration for all TCP targets regardless of scope. Use the local/external panels above when comparing scoped paths.

panels.title: TCP Phase Breakdown
panels.description: Instant snapshot of TCP probe phase durations pivoted by target. Status is the latest probe outcome from probe_success. tcp_connect is TCP connection establishment time; dns_resolve appears only for hostname targets. total_phases is the sum of observed TCP probe phases. Only Status and total_phases use background color; ordinary phase cells are uncolored. N/A cells mean the phase was not observed or does not apply.

targets.expr: label_replace(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="tcp"}, "phase", "Status", "__name__", ".*") or probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="tcp"} or label_replace(sum by (target_name) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="tcp"}), "phase", "total_phases", "__name__", ".*")

panels.title: TCP Phase Timing
panels.description: Per-phase timing for TCP probes. Hostname targets can emit dns_resolve; all reached TCP probes emit tcp_connect. Missing phases mean the latest probe did not reach that phase or the phase does not apply.

panels.title: DNS Status per Target
panels.description: Current DNS probe status per target. Status comes from probe_success, Resolve Time (s) from probe_dns_resolve_seconds, and Result Match from probe_dns_result_match when dns_expected is configured. N/A means result matching was not evaluated for that target.

panels.title: DNS Resolve Time
panels.description: DNS resolution time for DNS probes from probe_dns_resolve_seconds. This measures resolver lookup duration, not a generic UDP/TCP connectivity check.

panels.title: DNS Result Match
panels.description: Shows whether DNS resolution results match the configured dns_expected values. 1 = match, 0 = mismatch. Only populated for targets with dns_expected configured. Useful for detecting DNS hijacking or unexpected record changes.

panels.title: TLS Certificate Expiry — Earliest
panels.description: Days until the earliest TLS peer certificate expiry observed in the latest probe result. Targets where the latest probe failed without retrieving a certificate do not appear in this table.

panels.title: TLS Certificate Expiry — Chain
panels.description: Days until each certificate in the peer chain expires, including cert_index and cert_role labels from the latest probe result.

panels.title: TLS Cert Phase Breakdown
panels.description:
Instant snapshot of TLS certificate probe phase durations pivoted by target. Direct tls_cert probes can emit dns_resolve, tcp_connect, and tls_handshake. Proxy-path tls_cert probes emit proxy_dial, optional proxy_tls, proxy_connect, and tls_handshake; they do not send an HTTP request to the target.

Column legend:
- Target: TLS cert target name.
- Status: latest probe outcome from probe_success. OK = succeeded, FAIL = failed.
- dns_resolve: DNS lookup time for direct hostname targets.
- tcp_connect: TCP connection establishment time for direct probes.
- proxy_dial: TCP connection to the proxy, including proxy hostname DNS when applicable.
- proxy_tls: TLS handshake with an HTTPS proxy. N/A for plain HTTP proxies.
- proxy_connect: CONNECT request/response with the proxy.
- tls_handshake: TLS handshake with the target, directly or through the proxy tunnel.
- total_phases: sum of observed phase timings for this target.

Only Status and total_phases use background color. Ordinary phase cells are uncolored; N/A means the phase was not observed or does not apply.

targets.expr: sum by (target_name, phase) (label_replace(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="tls_cert"}, "phase", "Status", "__name__", ".*")) or sum by (target_name, phase) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="tls_cert"}) or label_replace(sum by (target_name) (probe_phase_duration_seconds{job=~"$job", probe_type="tls_cert"}), "phase", "total_phases", "__name__", ".*")

panels.title: TLS Phase Timing
panels.description: Per-phase timing for TLS certificate probes. Direct probes show DNS/TCP/TLS phases; proxy-path probes show proxy tunnel setup plus target TLS handshake. Missing phases mean the latest probe did not reach that phase or the phase does not apply.

panels.title: ICMP Status per Target
panels.description: Current ICMP probe status per target. Status is the latest probe outcome from probe_success. Avg RTT (s) is the average round-trip time across successful ICMP echo replies. Packet Loss is the latest loss ratio, where 0 means no loss and 1 means 100% loss. RTT Stddev (s) shows jitter across successful replies and is N/A when no replies were observed.
Total ICMP probe targets.
ICMP targets with 100% packet loss in the latest probe result.
Average ICMP echo round-trip time across targets that reported at least one reply.
ICMP targets with partial packet loss: loss greater than 0 and less than 100%.

panels.title: ICMP Avg RTT
panels.description: Average ICMP echo round-trip time per target. The series is present only when at least one ICMP echo reply was observed during the latest probe result.

panels.title: ICMP Packet Loss
panels.description: Packet loss ratio per target (0.0 = no loss, 1.0 = 100% loss). Calculated as (ping_count - received) / ping_count for each probe cycle. If the probe context expires before all pings are sent, unsent pings count as lost.

panels.title: ICMP RTT Stddev
panels.description: Population standard deviation of ICMP echo RTT. Values near zero are normal and indicate a stable network path. High stddev with normal average RTT indicates jitter or an unstable path.
Total MTU probe target/state series currently reported.
MTU targets whose current state is not ok. Inspect All MTU Probe States for the state/detail reason.
Minimum confirmed path MTU in bytes across MTU targets that reported an MTU value.

panels.title: All MTU Probe States
panels.description:
Current MTU probe result per target. Sorted by Status so failures appear first.

Column legend:
- Status: binary probe outcome from probe_success. OK = probe met its configured MTU policy, FAIL = it did not.
- Name / Service / Scope / Region / Target: target identity and routing context from config labels.
- State: MTU-specific diagnosis. ok = acceptable MTU confirmed, degraded = MTU below expected minimum or result is inconclusive, unreachable = target/path did not provide a usable MTU confirmation, error = probe/setup error.
- Detail: reason for the state, for example fragmentation_needed, largest_size_confirmed, larger_sizes_timed_out, all_sizes_timed_out, sanity_check_failed, or resolve_error.
- MTU (bytes): largest confirmed path MTU. N/A means no MTU value was confirmed in the latest probe result.

panels.title: Path MTU per Target
panels.description: Detected path MTU in bytes over time. Calculated as largest successful ICMP payload + 28 bytes (IP + ICMP headers). Standard Ethernet = 1500, WireGuard typical = 1420.

panels.title: MTU Current State
panels.description: Current MTU probe state counts by state/detail.
